methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $37M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Nuclear Energy (found 1 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 8 same-sector peers exist in Nuclear Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

private.name: Fuse Energy
private.rationale: Same sector (Nuclear Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $200M+

data.narrative:
Thea Energy is a Series A nuclear energy company founded by Brian Berzin that has raised $37M+ to date. The valuation analysis draws from a fragmented comparable universe: 3 public companies (OKLO, NNE, SMR) and 8 private peers including Fuse Energy, TerraPower, X-Energy, Kairos Power, and Oklo, though the private cohort spans heterogeneous stages and valuations.

A peer-median approach proved inapplicable due to insufficient clustering within narrow filters (same-stage, adjacent-stage, similar-tier combinations yielded fewer than 3 datapoints). The Stage-Progression method was therefore the primary triangulation lever, applying a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical of Series A advancement to the $37M raised proxy, yielding $74M. This single-method anchoring introduces methodological constraint but reflects available precision given the sparse peer set.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $63M to $85M, with a median of $74M. Key uncertainties include the absence of tight-stage comparables in nuclear energy, the heterogeneity of remaining private peers across technology substrates and development timelines, and sparse public trading multiples in the sector. Any final valuation should weight the thinness of the applicable dataset and consider management's forward guidance on capital efficiency and technology de-risking milestones.
